_about: self-media-platform project-level sandbox config. Mirrors atelier's yolo model: allow list is intentionally tiny (sandbox driver + observation tools); deny list is the wall for --dangerously-skip-permissions. The atelier VM for this project is named 'atelier-smp' to coexist with the parent project's 'atelier' VM.
_philosophy: Architecture is the wall, not the deny list. The host is supposed to be inert — every mutating op routes through bin/devbox into the atelier-smp VM. The allow list is the architectural surface; the deny list is the last-resort backstop for yolo mode. With --dangerously-skip-permissions, only the deny list still bites. The Python/web dev toolchain (uv, pip, node, pnpm, vite, docker) is pre-allowed because it lives inside the VM and is the natural surface for this project.
_write_scope: Write/Edit tools are NOT in the allow list, which means by default they prompt for any path. The project tree (./**) is auto-approved because the tool's defaults assume the project dir. To write elsewhere, the user must explicitly grant a path pattern.
_parallel_with_atelier: This project's atelier-smp VM is designed to run alongside the parent project's 'atelier' VM. Both share /Users/lazy/Code/crack/ via OrbStack's auto-share at /mnt/mac/. Each VM is a separate sandbox with its own state, env, and toolchain; they never modify each other.
